Off Calendar Aug-03-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (through Zoom):
Matthew Lavin, Esq. 212-397-1000 mlavin@napolilaw.com, John Tower, Esq. 760-436-5589 Towerlawsd@gmail.com, for Plaintiff.
Andrew Holly, Esq. 612-340-8830 Holly.Andrew@dorsey.com of Dorsey & Whitney LLP 612-340-8830 for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Plaintiff argues. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF BRISTOL SL HOLDINGS, INC., CEDAR CREEK RECOVERY, INC.'s Motion To Strike Defendant's First Affirmative Defense For Recoupment.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Bristol SL Holdings' motion to strike defendant United Behavioral Health's first affirmative defense is denied. Bristol fails to establish that the standard warranting a motion to strike has been met. (Code Civ. Proc. 436; see PH II, Inc. v. Superior Court (1995) 33 Cal.App.4th 1680, 1683 [use of motion to strike should be "cautious and sparing"].) To the extent that there are factual disputes regarding the bases for the defense, they cannot be decided on a motion to strike. (See word minutes for full details.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
